text:
Varuna (Lord of water), Wind, the Moon and Siva. The
water-veins in the different directions are named after their presiding
deities. There is the ninth known as the Great Vein in the centre
of these. There are hundreds of veins branching off from these
and bearing distinct appellations. A vein that comes up from the
nether-world is auspicious; so are those that are situated in the
four cardinal points, while those in the intermediate quarters are
inauspicious. Hereafter I shall explain the characteristics of these
veins.

If a rotang tree is seen flourishing in a waterless tract, there
will be water at a depth of cubits at a distance of 3 cubits to the
west of the tree. There will be found a westerly vein flowing under-
neath. At a depth of 2-| cubits the symptoms would be a pale white
frog; thereafter, yellow clay and further, a hard stone (which will
